questions.q: Which of the following configuration options is strictly restricted to container-level security contexts and cannot be declared at the pod-level?
questions.explanation: Linux 'capabilities' can only be added or dropped under a container-specific securityContext. Pod-level securityContexts support group, user, and fsGroup configurations, but do not support capabilities.

questions.q: What securityContext setting prevents processes running inside a container from modifying files in its root directory or writeable system paths?
questions.explanation: Setting 'readOnlyRootFilesystem: true' mounts the container's root directory as read-only, forcing the application to write data only to designated volumes or emptyDir mounts, drastically reducing the system attack surface.
